hero.title: Des webhooks qui
hero.subtitle: Envoyez des webhooks en toute confiance. Réessais automatiques, signatures HMAC, surveillance en temps réel. Pour les développeurs qui ont besoin de fiabilité.

features.hmacSignaturesDesc: Chaque webhook est signé avec SHA256. Vérifiez l'authenticité en un seul appel de fonction.
features.dashboardDesc: Surveillez les livraisons, suivez les taux de succès, déboguez les erreurs. Tout au même endroit.
features.lowLatencyDesc: Construit en Rust pour la vitesse. Surcharge sub-milliseconde sur la livraison de webhooks.
features.dlqDesc: Les livraisons échouées sont conservées pour le débogage. Rien ne se perd.
features.globalDesc: Déployez dans le monde entier pour une livraison de webhooks à faible latence.

configFailed: Échec de la création de la configuration
faqSubtitle: Tout ce que vous devez savoir sur HookSniff
a1: HookSniff est un service de livraison de webhooks pour les développeurs. Nous livrons les webhooks de manière fiable à vos points de terminaison avec des tentatives automatiques, des signatures HMAC et une surveillance en temps réel.
q2: Comment HookSniff se différencie de Svix ou Hookdeck ?
a2: HookSniff offre des fonctionnalités similaires à un coût 10x inférieur. Notre plan Pro est à 29\$/mois contre 490\$/mois pour Svix. Nous supportons également 11 SDKs, la livraison FIFO et CloudEvents.
q3: HookSniff est-il prêt pour la production ?
a3: Oui. HookSniff traite des milliers de livraisons de webhooks quotidiennement avec 99,9% de disponibilité. Nous utilisons PostgreSQL pour le stockage durable et des tentatives automatiques avec backoff exponentiel.
a4: Inscrivez-vous gratuitement sur hooksniff.vercel.app, obtenez votre clé API et créez un point de terminaison. Envoyez votre premier webhook en moins de 5 minutes.
q5: Ai-je besoin d'une carte de crédit pour commencer ?
a5: Non. Le plan gratuit comprend 10 000 livraisons de webhooks par mois sans carte de crédit. Passez à Pro ou Business selon vos besoins.
a6: Nous acceptons toutes les cartes de crédit via Stripe et supportons Polar.sh pour les paiements globaux. Tous les plans sont facturés mensuellement.
q7: Puis-je changer de plan à tout moment ?
a7: Oui. Mettez à niveau ou rétrogradez depuis votre tableau de bord. Les mises à niveau prennent effet immédiatement avec facturation au prorata. Les rétrogradations prennent effet à la fin de votre période de facturation actuelle.
q8: Que se passe-t-il si je dépasse les limites de mon plan ?
a8: Nous ne vous couperons pas. Si vous dépassez votre limite de webhooks, les livraisons supplémentaires sont mises en file d'attente et traitées lorsque votre limite est réinitialisée.
a9: Les livraisons échouées sont réessayées avec un backoff exponentiel : 30s, 60s, 120s, 5min, 10min, 30min. Après le nombre maximum de tentatives (par défaut 5), la livraison est déplacée vers la file d'attente des lettres mortes.
q10: Qu'est-ce que la vérification de signature HMAC ?
a10: Chaque livraison de webhook comprend un en-tête X-HookSniff-Signature contenant un hash HMAC-SHA256 du payload. Vérifiez cette signature pour vous assurer que le webhook provient de HookSniff.
a11: Oui. En plus de la livraison HTTP POST, HookSniff supporte les connexions WebSocket pour le streaming d'événements en temps réel.
q12: Qu'est-ce que la file d'attente des lettres mortes ?
a12: La file d'attente des lettres mortes stocke les livraisons échouées qui ont épuisé toutes les tentatives de réessai. Vous pouvez inspecter les payloads et les messages d'erreur avant de renvoyer la livraison.
a13: Oui. Toutes les données sont chiffrées en transit (TLS) et au repos. Nous utilisons HMAC-SHA256 pour les signatures de webhooks et Argon2id pour le hachage des mots de passe.
a14: Oui, pendant la durée de rétention de votre plan (7-365 jours). Les payloads sont stockés chiffrés et automatiquement supprimés après la période de rétention.
a15: Oui. Les clients du plan Business peuvent configurer des domaines personnalisés pour leurs points de terminaison de webhooks. Les certificats SSL sont automatiquement provisionnés via Let's Encrypt.
cantFindAnswer: Vous ne trouvez pas la réponse ? Notre équipe de support est là pour vous aider.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.heroSubtitle: Save 12+ months of webhook infrastructure development. Start free, scale when ready.
pricing.heroNote: No credit card required. No surprise charges. Cancel anytime.
pricing.payWhatYouUse: Only pay for what you actually send
pricing.payWhatYouUseDesc: Retries are free. Filtered messages are free. Failed deliveries that we retry 5 times still count as 1 webhook. We absorb the cost because reliable delivery is our promise.
pricing.roiDesc: See how much you save with HookSniff vs competitors.
buildOwnItems: Retry logic is hard to get right
pricing.startupDesc: We have special startup plans to help you grow. Pre-Series A startups get extended free tiers and significant discounts on paid plans.
pricing.ctaDesc: Join thousands of developers who trust HookSniff for webhook delivery.

pricingFaq.a: A webhook is an event delivered to your endpoint. Failed deliveries that are retried do not count as additional webhooks — retries are free. Filtered messages are also free.

pricingFaq.q: Do I need a credit card to start?
pricingFaq.a: No. You can sign up and start sending webhooks on the Free plan without a credit card. Upgrade when you need more volume or features.

pricingFaq.q: What happens if I exceed my plan limit?
pricingFaq.a: We never drop webhooks. If you exceed your monthly limit, additional events are metered at the per-plan rate. You can also set a hard cap in your dashboard settings.
Yes. A webhook includes all its retries. If a delivery fails and we retry it 3 times, that still counts as 1 webhook. We absorb the retry cost because reliable delivery is our core promise.
Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately and are prorated. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing cycle. No penalties, no hidden fees.
Yes! If you're an early-stage startup (pre-Series A), contact us at hello@hooksniff.com with your details. We offer significant discounts and extended free tiers.

pricingFaq.q: What's the difference between Pro and Business?
pricingFaq.a: Pro is for growing teams that need more volume and better monitoring. Business is for teams that need SLA guarantees, FIFO delivery, SSO, and dedicated support.
Yes. Annual billing saves you 20% compared to monthly. That's $278/year for Pro and $950/year for Business.

pricingFaq.q: What payment methods do you accept?
pricingFaq.a: We accept all major credit cards via Stripe. For Business and Enterprise plans, we also support invoicing and bank transfers.
Yes! HookSniff is open-source. You can self-host with Docker. Self-hosted instances get community support. Paid plans add priority support for self-hosted.
We support 11 SDKs: Node.js, Python, Rust, C#, Go, Swift, PHP, Elixir, Java, Kotlin, and Ruby.
Yes. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest. We use HMAC-SHA256 for webhook signatures, Argon2 for password hashing, and support 2FA/TOTP.
Yes. We are GDPR compliant. We process data in EU regions, support data export/deletion requests, and have a published privacy policy.
Free and Pro plans have a 99.9% uptime target (best effort). Business plans include a 99.9% uptime SLA with credits if we miss it.

pricingFaq.q: How does HookSniff compare to Svix?
pricingFaq.a: HookSniff offers similar features at 10x lower cost. Svix starts at $490/month; HookSniff Pro is $49/month. We also support more SDKs (11 vs 6), FIFO delivery, and CloudEvents.
Yes. You can export all your webhook logs, endpoint configurations, and delivery history as JSON or CSV from the dashboard.

transforms.title: 🔄 Webhook Transforms
transforms.subtitle: Filter, map, and enrich webhook payloads before delivery
transforms.selectEndpointHint: Select an endpoint to manage transforms
transforms.empty: No transform rules. Create one to start transforming payloads.

rateLimiting.title: ⚡ Rate Limiting
rateLimiting.subtitle: Monitor and configure rate limits for your webhook endpoints.
rateLimiting.emptyDesc: HookSniff automatically rate-limits webhook deliveries to protect your endpoints. Configure limits per endpoint in settings.
rateLimiting.tokenBucketDesc: Each endpoint has a token bucket that refills at the configured rate. Requests consume tokens.
rateLimiting.burstHandlingDesc: Short bursts are allowed up to the burst size, then requests are queued.
rateLimiting.queueRetryDesc: Excess requests are queued and delivered when capacity is available. Failed deliveries retry with exponential backoff.
rateLimiting.perEndpointConfigDesc: Each endpoint can have custom rate limits. Defaults: 10 req/sec, 600 req/min, burst 20.

sso.title: 🔐 SSO / SAML
sso.subtitle: Configure Single Sign-On for your organization. Enterprise plan required.
sso.enableSsoDesc: When enabled, all team members must authenticate via SSO.
sso.upgradeHintSuffix: to enable SSO for your organization.

auditLog.title: 📋 Audit Log
auditLog.subtitle: Track all activity in your workspace. Who did what, when, and from where.
auditLog.noActivityDesc: Actions like login, endpoint creation, API key rotation, and team changes will appear here.

routing.title: 🔀 Routing
routing.subtitle: Configure how webhooks are routed to your endpoints. Choose between round-robin, latency-based, or failover strategies.

customDomain.title: 🌐 Custom Domain
customDomain.subtitle: Use your own domain for the webhook portal. White-label your customers' experience.
customDomain.dnsRecordsDesc: Add these records to your DNS provider (Cloudflare, Route53, etc.)
customDomain.domainAdded: Domain added! Add the DNS records below.
customDomain.step1Desc: Enter the domain you want to use (e.g., webhooks.yourcompany.com)
customDomain.step2Desc: We'll give you CNAME and TXT records to add to your DNS provider
customDomain.step3Desc: We verify ownership and automatically provision an SSL certificate

signatureVerifier.title: 🔐 Signature Verifier
signatureVerifier.subtitle: Verify webhook signatures to ensure payloads are authentic. HookSniff signs every webhook with HMAC-SHA256.
signatureVerifier.validDesc: The payload is authentic and has not been tampered with.
signatureVerifier.invalidDesc: The signature does not match. The payload may have been tampered with.
signatureVerifier.step1Desc: When a webhook is delivered, HookSniff computes an HMAC hash of the raw request body using your endpoint's secret key.
signatureVerifier.step2Desc: The signature is sent in the x-hooksniff-signature header (format: sha256=<hex_digest>).
signatureVerifier.step3Desc: Compute the same HMAC hash on your server and compare it with the received signature. Use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.

endpointSettings.title: Endpoint Settings
endpointSettings.rotateSecretDesc: Rotate your signing secret. The old secret remains valid for 24 hours to allow seamless migration.
endpointSettings.testWebhookDesc: Send a test webhook to this endpoint to verify it's working correctly.
endpointSettings.testFailed: ❌ Failed — check endpoint URL and try again.
endpointSettings.rotateConfirmDesc: The old secret will remain valid for 24 hours. Update your webhook consumers to use the new secret before then.
endpointSettings.toastSecretRotated: Secret rotated! Old secret valid for 24 hours.
endpointSettings.toastTestSent: Test webhook sent! Check your endpoint.

schemasPage.noSchemasDesc: Register a schema to start validating your webhook payloads.

retryPolicy.title: 🔄 Retry Policy
retryPolicy.subtitle: Configure global retry behavior for webhook deliveries. Per-endpoint overrides take precedence.

retryPolicy.enableDlqDesc: Move permanently failed deliveries to DLQ
retryPolicy.retryOnStatusCodesDesc: Webhooks that return these HTTP status codes will be retried.
retryPolicy.delayPreviewDesc: How delays will look with current settings:
retryPolicy.tip: 💡 Per-endpoint retry policies override these global defaults. Configure them in Endpoint Settings.
